channel.title: Recent & Upcoming Talks | Casper van Elteren

item.title: Through the looking glass
item.description: Since the birth of classical physics, we have come to regard physics as the asnwer to our question on what reality “is”. Yet many systems we experience every day devies these laws of physics. In many of these systems systemic properties emerge through local interactions of its parts. These parts behind interact to bring forth something besides the parts. One promising venture is the use of information theory to describe the (emergent) behavior of various systems.In this talk I will explore the tension between the parts and the whole through the lens information theory.

item.title: What node is most important?
item.description: I was a guest speaker as part of the course Modeling and Data Analysis in Complex Networks. Additionally, the same slides were shown at the simulation based science group at the institute for advanced studies (IAS) at the university of Amsterdam. The talk concerned my research on the identification of driver-nodes in complex dynamical systems.
